mapper.sql: id, tenant_id, asset_code, asset_type, expected_by_local_time, timezone, stale_after_seconds, lookback_days, severity, enabled, created_at, updated_at
mapper.insert: insert into batch.asset_freshness_policy ( tenant_id, asset_code, asset_type, expected_by_local_time, timezone, stale_after_seconds, lookback_days, severity, enabled ) values ( #{p.tenantId}, #{p.assetCode}, #{p.assetType}, #{p.expectedByLocalTime}, #{p.timezone}, #{p.staleAfterSeconds}, #{p.lookbackDays}, #{p.severity}, #{p.enabled} ) on conflict (tenant_id, asset_code, asset_type) do update set expected_by_local_time = excluded.expected_by_local_time, timezone = excluded.timezone, stale_after_seconds = excluded.stale_after_seconds, lookback_days = excluded.lookback_days, severity = excluded.severity, enabled = excluded.enabled, updated_at = current_timestamp
mapper.update: update batch.asset_freshness_policy set asset_code = #{p.assetCode}, asset_type = #{p.assetType}, expected_by_local_time = #{p.expectedByLocalTime}, timezone = #{p.timezone}, stale_after_seconds = #{p.staleAfterSeconds}, lookback_days = #{p.lookbackDays}, severity = #{p.severity}, enabled = #{p.enabled}, updated_at = current_timestamp where tenant_id = #{p.tenantId} and id = #{p.id}
